subject: Computer Networking
title: Security Audits and Pen Testing
description: A quick, practical guide to Security Audits and Pen Testing.

hook.text: You think your network is secure. A pen tester breaks in within two hours. Now you know where the holes are.

buildup.text: Penetration testing simulates real attacks against your systems. Ethical hackers try to find what criminals would.

discovery.text: Security audits review configurations, policies, and code. They check compliance and identify systemic weaknesses.

twist.text: Companies often test only once a year. Attackers probe constantly. That gap is where breaches happen.

climax.text: Bug bounty programs invite the public to find flaws. Apple, Google, and Microsoft all pay for reported vulnerabilities.

punchline.text: Test yourself before attackers do. Find the holes first.

quiz.question: What is penetration testing?
options: Simulating real attacks to find vulnerabilities
Installing antivirus software on all devices
